canonical_name: the Gods which lie behind the Gods we know
the one human being she had ever wholly loved
the sons she had distanced from her
the world outside into which the young Arthur had ridden at Merlin's side
the dragon who has spread his wings over our coast
most men; and though there was little sound in the enclosed garden
the next High King of Britain
a land drowned before the ring stones on the Tor were raised
the rival kinglets all striving for the crown of the High King
a nobleman's dress, confronting the Christian priests
a Roman villa, and Arthur standing between two boys
two boys-one was her own Lancelet
the secret place where work like this must be done
a high hill overlooking all of Britain
a Christian land, and because when first the followers of the Christ
the King Stag who had died and shed his blood so that the champion might not die
the little dark smiths who had dwelled on the chalk before the ring stones were raised
the solemn-faced boy who walked now between the two Druids
the priestesses who brought him food
the priestesses who serve her in turns
a woman who petted me, then pushed me away
